text: IAEA confirms projectile struck auxiliary building near Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant — 4th attack on the facility. 1 physical protection staff killed. IAEA DG 'deeply concerned.' No radiation increase detected. Russia evacuated 198 more Rosatom staff (3rd wave), ~100 remain.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, UN News, Times of Israel confirmed.

text: Iranian cluster munitions landed near IDF Kirya HQ in Tel Aviv — 2 submunitions hit parking lot and nearby school. Multiple impacts across Ramat Gan, Bnei Brak, Petah Tikva. IDF confirms no injuries at HQ. Shows Iran still capable of hitting high-value targets despite air campaign.
verificationNote: Jerusalem Post, Times of Israel, Xinhua confirmed.

text: Iran military central command calls Trump's 48h ultimatum 'helpless, nervous, unbalanced and stupid' — warns 'gates of hell will open for you.' Ceasefire talks via Pakistan/Egypt/Turkey at standstill. Deadline expires Monday April 6. This is the most dangerous 24h window of the war.
verificationNote: Middle East Eye, Al Jazeera confirmed.

text: Kuwait Shuwaikh oil complex hit by drone — fire at Kuwait Petroleum Corp/Ministry of Oil building. Evacuated, contained, no injuries. Attribution unclear but likely Iran-aligned militia. Conflict spreading beyond main theatre — 3rd Gulf state oil facility targeted this week.
verificationNote: GMA News, Al Jazeera liveblog confirmed.

text: WaPo bombshell: Chinese AI firms (some with PLA ties) marketing detailed intelligence on US force movements in Iran. Using AI + open-source data to track carrier groups, aircraft, base activity. Strategic intel concern — data refining Chinese military capabilities in real time.
verificationNote: Washington Post confirmed investigation.

text: Day 37 summary: Bushehr 4th strike (1 killed). Mahshahr petrochem (5 killed, 170 wounded). Cluster munitions near Kirya HQ. Kuwait oil complex droned. Beirut Dahiyeh struck. Tyre hospital damaged (2 girls killed). Lebanon bridge destroyed. Iran rejects ultimatum. April 6 deadline TOMORROW. Brent $109.24/WTI $111.54. Gas $4.10.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, UN News, JPost, GMA News, Middle East Eye confirmed all items.

text: BREAKING: Missing F-15E WSO rescued via commando raid deep inside Iran near Isfahan. CIA ran deception campaign — told Iranians he was already captured. US set up secret airbase inside Iran, then destroyed 2 C-130s on the ground to prevent capture. Most daring SAR since Extortion 17.
verificationNote: CNN, Axios, NBC News, NPR, Al Jazeera all confirmed.

text: Iran claims it destroyed a US C-130 and 2 Black Hawks during Isfahan rescue op. Reality: US destroyed its own 2 stranded C-130s to prevent capture — Iran likely counting those. 1 Black Hawk confirmed hit but exited airspace. 5 Iranians killed in firefight. IRGC propaganda ≠ facts.
verificationNote: Anadolu Agency, Benzinga, Turkiye Today confirmed both sides' claims.

text: IDF conducted 272 strikes across 14 Iranian provinces in 24 hours. 184 killed. Beheshti University medical research institute near-destroyed. Over 30 Iranian universities targeted since war began. Academic infrastructure being systematically dismantled alongside military targets.
verificationNote: Haaretz, Jerusalem Post confirmed strike count and university targeting.

text: Hegseth fired Army Chief of Staff Gen. George + 2 other generals DURING wartime (April 2). Hegseth wants ground troops in Iran; Rubio wary. They're reportedly 'at each other's throats.' First Army CoS fired during active combat since MacArthur era.
verificationNote: Axios, CNN, Middle East Eye confirmed firings and Hegseth-Rubio feud.

text: Tel Aviv anti-war protest at Habima Square: ~1,000 gathered, police violently dispersed after court-imposed 600 cap. 17 arrested. Missile alert sounded DURING dispersal. Israeli public support for war at 45.5% — lowest since Feb 28. Domestic opposition growing.
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed protests, arrests, and missile alert timing.

text: Day 37 PM summary (3:32 AM PDT): F-15E WSO RESCUED (commando raid inside Iran); IDF 272 strikes/184 killed across 14 provinces; Iran claims C-130+2 Black Hawks destroyed; anti-war protests dispersed; Hegseth fired Army CoS; April 6 Hormuz deadline TOMORROW; Brent $109.24/WTI $111.54; gas $4.10.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNN, Axios, Haaretz, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel.

text: Neot Hovav chemical industrial zone hit AGAIN by Iranian ballistic missile (same plant struck in late March). Hazmat leak, 3 specialized teams deployed. 1 lightly wounded. Israel running out of targets to protect — Iron Dome interception rates declining as Iran increases salvo sizes.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog and Calcalist confirmed strike and hazmat response.

text: Gulf state attacks summary Apr 5: Kuwait — KPC HQ ablaze, 2 desalination plants damaged (90% of drinking water), Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery hit 3rd time. Bahrain — BAPCO storage tank fire. UAE — 9 BMs, 1 CM, 50 drones engaged. Iran systematically targeting civilian infrastructure.
verificationNote: The National, Gulf News, Al Jazeera, Khaleej Times confirmed all targets.

text: OPEC+ meeting today (Apr 5) deciding May production levels. May approve additional 411K b/d increase. But top OPEC producers (Saudi, Iraq, Kuwait, UAE) physically cannot export because Hormuz is closed. The irony: OPEC can vote to increase production but the oil has nowhere to go.
verificationNote: Business Standard, OPEC press release, Middle East Insider confirmed meeting and production dynamics.

text: Trump 48h Hormuz deadline expires TOMORROW (April 6, 8PM ET). Threatened "Power Plant Day and Bridge Day" for Tuesday. Iran military called it "helpless, nervous, unbalanced, stupid" and warned "gates of hell will open." Indirect talks via Vance-Ghalibaf channel (Pakistan mediating) at standstill.
verificationNote: CBS News, The Week, Al Jazeera, NPR confirmed deadline, threats, and rejection.

text: WTI-Brent inversion PERSISTS: WTI $111.54 above Brent $109.24. First sustained inversion since 2009. Market pricing deliverability over origin — US landlocked crude accessible while Brent-linked waterborne barrels blocked at Hormuz. Gold paradox: down 28% from pre-war peak despite active war. Strong dollar crushing metals.
verificationNote: Rigzone, Yahoo Finance, CNBC, Middle East Insider confirmed inversion and gold decline.

text: Day 37 EVE summary (5:27 AM PDT): F-15E WSO rescued; Neot Hovav hit again (hazmat); Bahrain BAPCO/UAE 60 projectiles; Kuwait droned; Pope Easter plea; Rubio arrests Soleimani family; Netanyahu "70% steel destroyed"; OPEC+ today; April 6 deadline TOMORROW; Brent $109.24/WTI $111.54; gas $4.10.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNN, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, Axios, Gulf News, Al-Monitor.

text: HAIFA: Iranian ballistic missile scored direct hit on residential building — NOT intercepted. Building partially collapsed. 4 injured, 3-4 missing under rubble including child. Warhead may not have detonated. Air defense gap significant — raises questions about Arrow/David's Sling coverage over Haifa corridor.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Haaretz, Anadolu Agency confirmed strike and casualties.

text: HORMUZ WATCH: Oman-Iran deputy FM talks exchanging transit proposals. 3 Omani ships transited outside Iran's 'approved corridor' — testing waters. French CMA CGM ship first Western vessel transit since war began (April 3). Cracks forming in blockade regime but core oil blockade holds.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, The National, UPI confirmed Oman talks and CMA CGM transit.

text: F-15E RESCUE DETAILS: WSO found in mountain crevice at 7,000 ft after 24+ hours evading IRGC. CIA ran deception campaign. 2 MC-130Js stuck in sand at improvised airstrip inside Iran — deliberately destroyed to prevent capture. 5 Iranians killed in extraction firefight. One of the most dramatic rescue ops since Jessica Lynch.
verificationNote: Fortune, JPost, NPR, FlightGlobal, The Aviationist confirmed rescue details.

text: ESCALATION WATCH: Trump Easter posts crossed new line — profanity-laden threat + revealed US arming Iranian dissidents via Kurds. Iran Gen. Aliabadi: 'gates of hell.' April 7 8PM ET deadline is the most specific yet. Houthis/IRGC/Hezbollah coordinated Ben Gurion attack. De-escalation window narrowing to hours.
verificationNote: NPR, Axios, Republic World, Anadolu Agency confirmed all referenced events.

text: LEBANON: 14 killed in Israeli strikes across Beirut/south — family of 6 among dead. Strike 100m from Rafik Hariri Hospital wounded 39. 272 IDF strikes across 14 Iranian provinces causing 184 casualties. Hezbollah conducted 51 attacks over weekend. War showing no signs of de-escalation despite Easter.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Alma Center daily report confirmed.

text: Day 37 PM summary (1:28 PM PDT): Haifa building struck (3 missing); Lebanon 14 killed; Houthis target Ben Gurion (intercepted); Trump 'Power Plant Day' Easter rant; Iran 'gates of hell'; Oman-Iran Hormuz talks; Markets closed Easter; Brent $109.24/WTI $111.54; gas $4.10; April 7 deadline 2 DAYS.
verificationNote: Compiled from Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, NPR, Axios, Republic World, CNN.
